notes: Most prevalent ESBL globally; hydrolyzes cefotaxime, ceftriaxone
Second most common CTX-M variant worldwide
Emerging CTX-M variant in E. coli ST131 C2 clade
Metallo-beta-lactamase; carbapenem resistance; WHO critical priority. Enterobacterales-wide (plasmid-borne); validated in E. coli reference.
NDM variant with enhanced activity; emerging in community E. coli. Enterobacterales-wide (plasmid-borne).
Serine carbapenemase; meropenem/imipenem resistance. Enterobacterales-wide; dominant in K. pneumoniae, transferable to E. coli/Enterobacter.
KPC variant; Enterobacterales-wide. crRNA designed against conserved KPC core; detects in any host species.
Carbapenemase with weak carbapenem hydrolysis; hard to detect phenotypically. Enterobacterales-wide (plasmid-borne).
Primary FQ resistance mutation; Ser83→Leu in QRDR
Second-step FQ resistance mutation; with S83L = high-level resistance
Topoisomerase IV mutation; contributes to high-level FQ resistance
parC QRDR mutation; auxiliary FQ resistance determinant
Promoter mutation increasing AmpC expression; cephalosporin resistance
